more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
more.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist's day-long procession through the city's campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice.

more.title: The Reversal Reality: Why Zimbabwe’s “Correction” Is a White Flag in Disguise
more.description: By Samuel Musarika For decades, the word “irreversible” was the cornerstone of Zimbabwe’s land reform politics. It was used as a shield against international criticism, a rallying cry for sovereignty, and a warning to anyone who questioned the legality or consequences of the land seizures that began in 2000. Yet in May 2026, Agriculture Minister [...]

more.title: Zimbabwe tightens grip on raw mineral exports
more.description: Zimbabwe has reiterated its commitment to banning the export of unprocessed minerals as government and lawmakers intensify pressure to ensure the country fully benefits from its vast mineral resources through value addition and beneficiation. The renewed policy push comes amid growing concern in Parliament that Zimbabwe continues exporting raw minerals such as chrome, lithium, platinum [...] The post Zimbabwe tightens grip on raw mineral exports appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Zimnat ordered to pay US$1,4m claim
more.description: The High Court has ordered Zimnat General Insurance to pay a combined US$2,46 million to tourism operators after ruling that Covid-19 triggered valid business interruption cover under insurance policies issued during the pandemic.
